title: Silky Smooth Mexican Poblano Tinga
description: A fresh and fluffy one-pot Mexican dish featuring roasted poblano peppers, basil, and a hint of cream, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: Roast the poblano peppers in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes, then peel and chop them.
Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the chopped basil and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the roasted poblano peppers, heavy cream, and shredded cheese to the pot.
Whisk the mixture until smooth and creamy.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the tinga for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the silky smooth Mexican poblano tinga hot, garnished with additional basil if desired.
